titleStmt.title: SII 23.578: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 578. (A.R. No. 578 of 1907.) Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District Saumyanātha temple—on the north base of the same maṇḍapa, 1st tier Kulōttuṅga III year 26: 1202-03 A.D.

summary.p: The King is given the appellation “who took Madurai and the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya”. The inscription gives the cyclic year Raktākshi and month Āṉi and registers the gift of three villages as agrahāra exempting them from taxes like silvari and peruvari, by Nallaṉ Siddharasa, son of Madhurāntaka Pottappichchōḻa alias Eṟama-Siddharasa. These villages were Nirandanūr alias Kulōttuṅgaśōḻachaturvēdimaṅgalam, Āṇḍappūr alias Tyāgasamudra-chaturvēdimaṅgalam and Mantram alias Bhujabalasiddhi-chaturvēdimaṅgalam.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
jayan=ti siddharājasya caṇḍādordaṇḍavikramāḥ viśvaviśvambharā rakṣādīkṣāddhvaradhura ndharāḥ—tiruvāykkeḻvimuṉṉuṭaittāka maturaiyum pāṇṭiyaṉ muṭittalaiyuṅ koṇṭaruḷiya tribhuvaṉaccakravattikaḷ śrīkulottuṅka coḻa tevaṟku yāṇṭu 2 10 6 Āvatāṉa raktākṣi samvatsarattu Āṉi māsa madhirājendramaṇṭalattu meṟpākkai niran=tanūrāṉa Akara brahmadeyam śrīkulottuṅkacoḻac catuṟpetimaṅkalamum Āṇṭappūrāṉa tyāgasa mudrac catuṟpetimaṅkalamum mantramāṉa bhujabalasiddhic catuṟpetimaṅ kalamum Immūṉṟūrum maturān=takap pottippic coḻaṉāṉa Eṟamasiddha rasar piḷḷai
nallaṉ siddharasaneṉ candrādityavarai Eppeṟpaṭṭa cilvari peruvari taviṟntu Akaramākak kuṭutteṉ Itukku Aḻivuceyvār gaṅkaik karai Āyiraṅ kurāṟpacuk kuttiṉāṉ pāpattilp paṭuvār-ippaṭik kivai nallaṉ siddharasaneḻuttu-ippaṭikkivai talaiyārip puṭoli nāyakka neḻuttu ..
Digital edition of SII 23.578 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
